evals.prompt: I have an iOS app in Xcode. It builds fine, but when I try to validate it on simulator, xcodebuild fails with 'No scheme found'. How do I debug this?
evals.expected_output: Response should invoke ios-validation and point to Pre-flight checks (lines 22). First: 'xcodebuild -list' to see available schemes. If scheme is missing, it's not registered in the .xcodeproj file. User must add scheme in Xcode: Product → Scheme → Edit Scheme, or use 'xcodebuild -scheme MyAppName' with exact name from -list output. Then retry Build step (lines 32-56).

evals.prompt: I built and installed the app on simulator. It launched and rendered the main screen. Now I need to test a deep link: myapp://item/123. Walk me through capturing evidence that the deep link actually works.
evals.expected_output: Response should invoke ios-validation, Step 7 (lines 119-140). (1) Get booted simulator UDID. (2) Open deep link: 'xcrun simctl openurl $UDID "myapp://item/123"'. (3) Wait 2 seconds. (4) Screenshot: 'xcrun simctl io $UDID screenshot e2e-evidence/ios-deeplink-item-123.png'. (5) Open the screenshot and describe: does it show the item detail screen? If deep link doesn't work, screenshot shows wrong screen (e.g., home screen instead of detail). THAT'S evidence.
